Lore Item
Slot: HEAD
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 5
MANA: +10
SV DISEASE: +10 SV POISON: +10
Effect: Gather Shadows (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 5.0) at Level 20
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: SHD NEC
Race: HUM ERU DEF TRL OGR GNM IKS FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

# Jun 19 2011 at 2:15 AM Rating: Decent
rgriffith3000
3 posts
I can confirm the pre-nerf circlet can be cast on the run. It is basically instant cast and a great item.
▲ Page top
What's the difference between this and pre-nerf?
QuoteReply
# Nov 28 2009 at 4:45 PM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
Is the only difference between this and the pre-nerf version the race tags? That's the only difference I can see. The pre-nerf says ALL, while this one states which races can use it. All the other stats remain the same. If that's the only difference then why was there such an outcry when they changed it? It looks like everyone who could use it before can still use it and it has the same stats and abilities.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
What's the difference between this and pre-nerf?
QuoteReply
# Nov 29 2009 at 2:01 PM Rating: Decent
nyteshayd
View User Forum
Scholar
530 posts
There was a very important change between the pre-nerf Circlet of Shadow and this. Aside from what you've already noticed, the original CoS had a cast time of 0.1 seconds, which is near-instant and fast enough that you could cast on the run, I've heard (haven't confirmed it myself, don't have anything with the same cast time).

This one has a 5.0 second cast time, completely eliminating that option and limiting the usefulness to "neat item" instead of "essential piece," since both of the classes that can use it get the exact same spell at a reasonably low level.

Edited, Nov 29th 2009 3:04pm by nyteshayd
____________________________
Jiriki Sa`Onserei, 120/67k AA Wood Elf Ranger
Anadriel Starfire, 120/46k AA High Elf Mage

What's the difference between this and pre-nerf?
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2011 at 2:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592343951762

Scholar
44 posts
Yes you can infact use the pre-nerf CoS while running/mounted. Very useful, not sure wether or not necs/SKs got any instant invis aa or not yet. At least it used to be immensly useful.
▲ Page top
What's the difference between this and pre-nerf?
QuoteReply
# Sep 20 2012 at 12:39 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1715559370129
View User Forum
169 posts
Can't say for Necros, I assume they have same AA, but SKs get an AA called Cloak of Shadows, and it is insta cast. Since SKs are a Necro hybrid, I would assume that Necros get a similiar spell. The downside is its a Class AA, and required level 70 to purchase.
▲ Page top
my experience
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2007 at 11:15 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592345655352
Sage
237 posts
I've picked two of these up in the last month or so. I've been able to spawn the goblin bodyguard three times, two of which dropped the circlet. He's spawned in the same place each time, which is in the room where Chief RokGus spawns. As you enter the mines, take a right, then a left to a big circular room. I found that I was able to spawn him by simply clearing the room repeatedly, although I did clear the static spawns in the hallway without seeing him there. Spawn times seemed on the order of 20 mins or so.

Scholar
429 posts
No, only Necro and SK can use it even from inventory only. Any other class will get a message that your race, class, or diety can't use it (or something like that)

# Jan 06 2003 at 5:17 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361918778
12 posts
the pre nerfed one is actually called "circlet of shadow" (not plural) its insta cast invis (.1 cast time is the same as insta cast), the nerfed one called "circlet of shadows" and has a 5 sec casting time

▲ Page top
godly
QuoteReply
# Mar 04 2002 at 8:08 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771555895
8 posts
this item is godly. insta-invis.. very glad i bought mine for 1.35k way before they nerfed it.. esp good for bad pulls... fiegn... wait a few... stand, invis.. bamo, no aggro.. its wonderful... i really hope they make the prenerf no-drop so i am not tempted to sell mine.. its also good for when your invis is about to break when on the 2nd floor of hate, trying to find your way down after a wipe-out :) also... when i bought mine, i was only level 19, i didnt know when it click'd so when i ding'd 20, to my surprise, it worked. getting inst-invis 10 levels before you are supposed to is wonderful :) every true good sk puller should have one.. or any that do CRs alot :) me thinks this is an item that makes SKs the best pullers :) ok.. enought with my rambling... if you have one.. hold on to it.. if you dont.. buy one..
▲ Page top
No, you just think your godly
QuoteReply
# Jul 31 2002 at 2:37 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591769682424
44 posts
Actually, the best sk pullers don't need an item like this to be awsome pullers .. the pre-nerfs just let any halfass sk seem like and think that they are a good puller, and thats a major reason it was nerfed along with the fact that they were waaaaaaay over farmed (and a cheezy item on PvP). They are nice to have though, but won't make you a better player, only skills and true experiance will do that.

These never improved my pulling ability, not even by a bit .. sold mine for 30k (and i'm still one of the very best pullers around, unlike the guy that bought the pre-nerf... he still sucked)

The nerfed ones are only good for freeing up a spell slot and giving sk twinks invis 10 levels before they should have it (unless that was nerfed aswell)
▲ Page top
